Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of burial mounds on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]]

Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]]

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of burial mounds on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq deprecated open]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qqqq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]]

According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of burial mounds on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]]

Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of burial mounds on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:New]]

Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of burial mounds on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:pgoodbye $1]][[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:pgoodbye $1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}

During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}

{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}} [[Category:New]]

Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:Removing this excess]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]]

It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also! {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | Alternate names = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:11]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]]

Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|12=draft}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|12=draft}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|12=draft}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|12=draft}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|12=draft}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|12=draft}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|12=draft}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|12=draft}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|12=draft}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|12=draft}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|12=draft}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|12=draft}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|12=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=draft}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|12=draft}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}

It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|12=draft}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|12=draft}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|12=draft}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|12=draft}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|12=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=draft}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|12=draft}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:return the see also]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}
