{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|12=draft}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|12=draft}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|12=draft}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|12=draft}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|12=draft}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|12=draft}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|12=draft}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|12=draft}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|12=draft}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|12=draft}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|12=draft}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|12=draft}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|12=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=draft}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|12=draft}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}

Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|12=draft}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|12=draft}} and was ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|12=draft}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|12=draft}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|12=draft}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|12=draft}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|12=draft}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|12=draft}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|12=draft}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|12=draft}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|12=draft}}, the shrine received a rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|12=draft}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|12=draft}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|12=draft}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|12=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј|12=draft}}, the shrine was ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=draft}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|12=draft}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}

In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=draft|12=draft}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}

{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|12=draft|12=draft}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|12=draft|12=draft}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|12=draft|12=draft}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|12=draft|12=draft}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|12=draft|12=draft}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|12=draft|12=draft}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|12=draft|12=draft}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|12=draft|12=draft}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|12=draft|12=draft}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|12=draft|12=draft}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|12=draft|12=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=draft|12=draft}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|12=draft|12=draft}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|12=draft|12=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}}

revision.text:
{{зҘһзӨҫ |еҗҚз§° = й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ |з”»еғҸ = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
жӢқж®ҝ |жүҖеңЁең° = [[иҢЁеҹҺзңҢ]][[еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ]]е№Ўз”ә539 |з·ҜеәҰеәҰ = 36 | з·ҜеәҰеҲҶ = 32 | з·ҜеәҰз§’ = 9 | N(еҢ—з·Ҝ)еҸҠгҒіS(еҚ—з·Ҝ) = N |зөҢеәҰеәҰ = 140 |зөҢеәҰеҲҶ = 32 | зөҢеәҰз§’ = 43 | E(жқұзөҢ)еҸҠгҒіW(иҘҝзөҢ) = E |зҘӯзҘһ = з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ
еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ |зҘһдҪ“ = |зӨҫж ј = [[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]пјҲе°Ҹпјү
ж—§[[йғ·зӨҫ]] |еүөе»ә = пјҲдјқпјүз¬¬10д»Ј[[еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ]]е№ҙй–“ |жң¬ж®ҝ = [[жөҒйҖ ]] |еҲҘеҗҚ = |жңӯжүҖзӯү = |дҫӢзҘӯ = [[4жңҲ9ж—Ҙ]] |зҘһдәӢ = |ең°еӣі = Japan Ibaraki |гғ©гғҷгғ«дҪҚзҪ®=left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|йіҘеұ…]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|жң¬ж®ҝ]] '''й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ'''пјҲгҒӘгҒҢгҒҜгҒҹгҒ№гҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјүгҒҜгҖҒ[[иҢЁеҹҺзңҢ]][[еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ]]гҒ«гҒӮгӮӢ[[зҘһзӨҫ]]гҖӮ[[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]гҒ§гҖҒ[[иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|ж—§зӨҫж ј]]гҒҜ[[йғ·зӨҫ]]гҖӮ == зҘӯзҘһ == зҘӯзҘһгҒҜд»ҘдёӢгҒ®2жҹұгҒ§гҖҒгҖҺ[[еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ]]гҖҸгҒ«иЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢй•·е№ЎйғЁдёҖж—ҸгҒ®зҘ–зҘһгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢзҘһзӨҫз”ұз·’жӣёгҖӮгҖӮ * '''з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ''' пјҲгҒӢгӮҖгҒҜгҒҹгҒІгӮҒгҒ®гҒҝгҒ“гҒЁпјү * '''еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ''' пјҲгҒҹгҒҰгҒ®гҒҝгҒ“гҒЁпјү == жӯҙеҸІ == === й•·е№ЎйғЁгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰ === зӨҫеҗҚгҒ«гҒӮгӮӢгҖҢй•·е№ЎгҖҚгҒЁгҒҜзө№з№”зү©гҒ®дёҖзЁ®гғ»[[зөҒ|зөҒпјҲгҒӮгҒ—гҒҺгҒ¬пјү]]гӮ’жҢҮгҒҷиЁҖи‘үгҒ§гҖҒгҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁгҖҚгҒЁгҒҜгҒқгӮҢгӮ’з№”гӮӢжҠҖиЎ“иҖ…йӣҶеӣЈгӮ’иЎЁгҒҷгҖӮж–ҮзҢ®дёҠгҒ®й•·е№ЎйғЁж°ҸгҒ«гҒҜгҖҒзҡҮеҲҘж°Ҹж—ҸгҒЁжёЎжқҘзі»ж°Ҹж—ҸгҒҢиҰӢгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҖӮгҖҺ[[ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ]]гҖҸйҖёж–ҮгҒ®[[йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|йҳҝжҷәзҺӢ]]жқЎгҒ§гҒҜгҖҒй•·е№ЎйғЁгҒ®зҘ–гҒҜеё°еҢ–гҒ—гҒҹгҖҢ[[дёғе§“жјўдәә]]гҖҚгҒ®гҒҶгҒЎзҡҖпјҲгҒ“гҒҶпјүе§“гҒ§гҖҒжң«иЈ”гҒ«дҪҗжіўеӨҡжқ‘дё»пјҲгҒ•гҒҜгҒҹгҒ®гҒҷгҒҗгӮҠпјүгҒҢгҒ„гӮӢгҒЁиЁҳгҒҷгҖӮгҒҫгҒҹгҖҺ[[еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ]]гҖҸ[[й–ӢеҢ–еӨ©зҡҮ]]ж®өгҒ«гӮҲгӮҢгҒ°гҖҒ[[еҪҰеқҗзҺӢ|ж—ҘеӯҗеқҗзҺӢ]]пјҲй–ӢеҢ–еӨ©зҡҮз¬¬3зҡҮеӯҗпјүгҒ®еӯҗгғ»[[зҘһеӨ§ж №зҺӢ]]пјҲгҒӢгӮҖгҒҠгҒҠгҒӯгҒ®гҒҚгҒҝпјүгҒҢй•·е№ЎйғЁгҒ®зҘ–гҒЁгҒ—гҖҒзҫҺжҝғгҒ®[[жң¬е·ЈеӣҪйҖ ]]гҒЁеҗҢж—ҸгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҖӮ === еүөе»ә === еҪ“зӨҫгҒ®еүөе»әгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҒгҖҺ[[еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ]]гҖҸ[[д№…ж…ҲйғЎ]]жқЎгҒ«гҒҜгҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁгҒ®зӨҫгҖҚгҒ«й–ўгҒҷгӮӢиЁҳдәӢгҒҢијүгӮӢгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮӢгҒЁгҖҒзҸ еЈІзҫҺдёҮе‘ҪпјҲгҒҷгӮҒгҒҝгҒҫгҒ®гҒҝгҒ“гҒЁпјүзҡҮеӯ«пјҲгҒҷгӮҒгҒҝгҒҫпјүгҖҒгҒҷгҒӘгӮҸгҒЎ[[гғӢгғӢгӮ®|з“Ҡз“Ҡжқөе°Ҡ]]гӮ’жҢҮгҒҷгҖӮгҒҢеӨ©гҒӢгӮүйҷҚиҮЁгҒ—гҒҹйҡӣгҒ«з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘ҪгҒҢеҫ“гҒ„гҖҒ[[ж—Ҙеҗ‘еӣҪ|ж—Ҙеҗ‘]]гҒӢгӮү[[зҫҺжҝғеӣҪ|зҫҺжҝғ]]гҒ«иҮігҒЈгҒҹгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҖӮгҒқгҒ—гҒҰ[[еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ]]гҒ®еҫЎдё–гҒ«й•·е№ЎйғЁгҒ®йҒ зҘ–гғ»еӨҡеј–е‘ҪгҒҢзҫҺжҝғгҒӢгӮүд№…ж…ҲгҒ«йҒ·гӮҠгҖҒж©ҹж®ҝгӮ’е»әгҒҰгҒҰеҲқгӮҒгҒҰз№”гҒЈгҒҹгҒЁдјқгҒҲгӮӢгҖӮ еҪ“зӨҫгҒҜеҸӨеўізҫӨпјҲе№ЎеұұеҸӨеўізҫӨпјүгҒ®дёӯгҒ«дҪҚзҪ®гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒҢгҖҒгҒ“гҒ®еҸӨеўізҫӨгҒҜеҪ“зӨҫгҒ®зҘӯзҘҖж°Ҹж—ҸгҒ®гӮӮгҒ®гҒЁиҰӢгӮүгӮҢгҖҒгҒқгҒ®еҮәеңҹе“ҒгҒӢгӮүеҪ“зӨҫгҒӘгӮүгҒігҒ«зҘӯзҘҖж°Ҹж—ҸгҒ®жҖ§ж јгҒҢжҢҮж‘ҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖӮ === жҰӮеҸІ === гҖҺеёёйҷёиӘҢж–ҷйғЎйғ·иҖғгҖҸгҒ«гӮҲгӮҢгҒ°гҖҒ[[зҘһйҡҺ]]гҒҜ[[д»ҒеҜҝ]]е…ғе№ҙпјҲ[[851е№ҙ]]пјүгҒ«жӯЈе…ӯдҪҚдёҠгҖҺж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІгҖҸд»ҒеҜҝе…ғе№ҙпјҲ851е№ҙпјүжӯЈжңҲ27ж—ҘжқЎгҒ«гҒҜгҖҒеӨ©дёӢгҒ®и«ёзҘһгҒ«еҜҫгҒ—гҒҰжңүдҪҚз„ЎдҪҚгӮ’е•ҸгӮҸгҒҡжӯЈе…ӯдҪҚдёҠгҒ«еҸҷгҒҷгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶиЁҳдәӢгӮ’ијүгҒӣгӮӢгҖӮгҖҒгҒ®гҒЎ10еәҰгҒ®иҙҲдҪҚгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠ[[жҳҺеҝң]]10е№ҙпјҲ[[1501е№ҙ]]пјүгҒ«жӯЈдёүдҪҚгҒ«йҒ”гҒ—гҒҹгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҖҺиҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®ең°еҗҚгҖҸй•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫй …гҖӮгҖӮ[[е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј]]дёӯжңҹгҒ®гҖҺ[[е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі]]гҖҸгҒ«гҒҜгҖҢ[[еёёйҷёеӣҪ]]д№…ж…ҲйғЎ й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳијүгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒ[[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]гҒ«еҲ—гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒӘгҒҠгҖҒгҖҺе»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёігҖҸгҒ§гҒҜ[[жӯҰи”өеӣҪ]][[иіҖзҫҺйғЎ]]гҒ«гӮӮеҗҢеҗҚгҒ®[[й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ]]гӮ’ијүгҒӣгӮӢгҖӮгҖӮеёёйҷёеӣҪгҒЁй•·е№ЎйғЁгҒ®й–ўдҝӮгҒ«й–ўгҒ—гҒҰгҒҜгҖҒгҖҺ[[е»¶е–ңејҸ]]гҖҸдё»иЁҲејҸгҒ«гҒҜеёёйҷёеӣҪгҒ®иӘҝгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁ7з–ӢгҖҚгҒ®иЁҳијүгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢгҒ»гҒӢгҖҒгҖҺ[[йЎһиҒҡеӣҪеҸІ]]гҖҸе·»54гҒ§[[ејҳд»Ғ]]8е№ҙпјҲ[[817е№ҙ]]пјүгҒ«гҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁзҰҸиүҜеҘігҖҚгҒ®еҗҚгҒҢиҰӢгҒҲгӮӢгҖҺж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ…гҖҸй•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫй …гҖӮгҖӮ [[еә·е№і]]е№ҙй–“пјҲ[[1058е№ҙ]]-[[1065е№ҙ]]пјүгҒ«[[жәҗй јзҫ©]]гҒҢеҘҘзҫҪеҮәе…өгҒ®йҡӣгҒ«жҲҺж——1ж——гӮ’еҘүзҙҚгҒ—гҒҰжҲҰеӢқзҘҲйЎҳгӮ’иЎҢгҒ„гҖҒеҮұж—ӢжҷӮгҒ«й№ҝеі¶гғ»дёүеі¶гғ»зҘһжҳҺгғ»иӢҘе®®гҒ®гҖҢеӣӣжүҖжҳҺзҘһгҖҚгӮ’еӢ§и«ӢгҒ—гҒҹгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®еӣӣжүҖжҳҺзҘһгҒҢзӣӣеӨ§гҒ«гҒӘгҒЈгҒҰгҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒ®зӨҫеҸ·гӮ’еӨұгҒ„гҖҒгҒ•гӮүгҒ«гҒ®гҒЎгҒ«гҒҜгҖҢй№ҝеі¶жҳҺзҘһгҖҚгҒЁз§°гҒҷгӮӢгҒ®гҒҝгҒ«гҒӘгҒЈгҒҹгҒЁдјқгҒҲгӮӢгҖӮ [[дёӯдё–]]д»ҘйҷҚгҒҜгҖҢе°Ҹе№Ўи¶іжҳҺзҘһгҖҚгҒ®гҒЎгҖҢй§’еҪўзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁгҒ„гҒЈгҒҹгҖӮ[[е»¶дә«]]е№ҙй–“пјҲ[[1744е№ҙ]]-[[1748е№ҙ]]пјүгҒ«иҮігӮҠгҖҒеҸӨиҖҒгҒ®еҸЈзў‘гҒ«гӮҲгӮҠж—§еҸ·гҒ®гҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒ«еҫ©гҒ—гҒҹгҖӮеҪ“зӨҫгҒҜ[[ж°ҙжҲёи—©]]гҒӢгӮүгҒ®еҙҮж•¬гӮӮзҜӨгҒҸгҖҒйҷӨең°4зҹігҒҢиЁұгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒҹгҖӮ [[жҳҺжІ»]]6е№ҙпјҲ[[1873е№ҙ]]пјүгҖҒ[[иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ]]гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰ[[йғ·зӨҫ]]гҒ«еҲ—гҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ == й–ўдҝӮең° == * ж—§е®®и·Ў *: еҪ“зӨҫгҒ®ж—§зӨҫең°гҒ§гҖҒеҢ—иҘҝзҙ„700гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гҒ«дҪҚзҪ®гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮзҘһијҝгҒҢеҮәгӮӢйҡӣгҒҜеҝ…гҒҡгҒ“гҒ“гҒ«е®үзҪ®гҒҷгӮӢгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҖӮ * е№ЎеұұеҸӨеўізҫӨ *: еҪ“зӨҫгҒ®йҺ®еә§гҒҷгӮӢеҸ°ең°дёҠгҒ«гҒӮгӮӢеҸӨеўізҫӨгҒ§гҖҒ10ж•°еҹәгҒ®еҸӨеўігҒӢгӮүгҒӘгӮӢгҖӮеӨҡгҒҸгҒ®еүҜи‘¬е“ҒгҒҢеҮәеңҹгҒ—гҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒеёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®еҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮеҪ“зӨҫгӮ’еҘүж–ҺгҒ—гҒҹж°Ҹж—ҸгҒЁгҒ®й–ўдҝӮгҒҢжҢҮж‘ҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖҺиҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®ең°еҗҚгҖҸе№ЎеұұеҸӨеўізҫӨй …гҖӮгҖӮ == зҘӯдәӢ == * е…ғж—ҰзҘӯ пјҲ1жңҲ1ж—ҘпјүзҘӯдәӢгҒҜзҘһзӨҫз”ұз·’жӣёгҒ«гӮҲгӮӢгҖӮ * зҘҲе№ҙзҘӯ пјҲ2жңҲ20ж—Ҙпјү * жҳҘдҫӢзҘӯ пјҲ4жңҲ9ж—Ҙпјү * з§ӢеӯЈзҘӯ пјҲж—§жҡҰ9жңҲ29ж—Ҙпјү * ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ пјҲ11жңҲ30ж—Ҙпјү == зҸҫең°жғ…е ұ == '''жүҖеңЁең°''' * [[иҢЁеҹҺзңҢ]][[еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ]]е№Ўз”ә539 '''е‘Ёиҫә''' * е№ЎеұұеҸӨеўізҫӨ == и„ҡжіЁ == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} '''жіЁйҮҲ''' {{reflist|group="жіЁ"}} '''еҮәе…ё''' {{reflist}} == еҸӮиҖғж–ҮзҢ® == * зҘһзӨҫз”ұз·’жӣёгҖҒеўғеҶ…иӘ¬жҳҺжқҝ * гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚдҪ“зі» иҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®ең°еҗҚгҖҸпјҲ[[е№іеҮЎзӨҫ]]пјүеёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫй … * еҢ—з• е…ӢзҫҺгҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫгҖҚпјҲ[[и°·е·қеҒҘдёҖ]] з·ЁгҖҺж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°- 11 й–ўжқұгҖҸпјҲ[[зҷҪж°ҙзӨҫ]]гҖҒ1984е№ҙпјүпјү * гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨд»Јж°Ҹж—ҸдәәеҗҚиҫһе…ё жҷ®еҸҠзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ]]гҖҒ2010е№ҙпјүй•·е№ЎйғЁж°Ҹй … == й–ўйҖЈй …зӣ® == {{Commonscat|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}} * [[й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ]] пјҲ[[еҹјзҺүзңҢ]][[е…җзҺүйғЎ]][[дёҠйҮҢз”ә]]пјү - [[жӯҰи”өеӣҪ]][[иіҖзҫҺйғЎ]]гҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫгҖӮ {{зҘһйҒ“ жЁӘ}}{{иҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®зҘһзӨҫдёҖиҰ§зӣ®ж¬Ў}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒӘгҒӢгҒҜгҒҹгҒёгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} [[Category:иҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®зҘһзӨҫ]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӣҪгҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]] [[Category:иҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®ж—§йғ·зӨҫ]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]]

'''й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ'''пјҲгҒӘгҒҢгҒҜгҒҹгҒ№гҒҳгӮ“гҒҳгӮғпјүгҒҜгҖҒ[[иҢЁеҹҺзңҢ]][[еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ]]гҒ«гҒӮгӮӢ[[зҘһзӨҫ]]гҖӮ[[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]гҒ§гҖҒ[[иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ|ж—§зӨҫж ј]]гҒҜ[[йғ·зӨҫ]]гҖӮ == зҘӯзҘһ == зҘӯзҘһгҒҜд»ҘдёӢгҒ®2жҹұгҒ§гҖҒгҖҺ[[еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ]]гҖҸгҒ«иЁҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢй•·е№ЎйғЁдёҖж—ҸгҒ®зҘ–зҘһгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢзҘһзӨҫз”ұз·’жӣёгҖӮгҖӮ * '''з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ''' пјҲгҒӢгӮҖгҒҜгҒҹгҒІгӮҒгҒ®гҒҝгҒ“гҒЁпјү * '''еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ''' пјҲгҒҹгҒҰгҒ®гҒҝгҒ“гҒЁпјү == жӯҙеҸІ == === й•·е№ЎйғЁгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰ === зӨҫеҗҚгҒ«гҒӮгӮӢгҖҢй•·е№ЎгҖҚгҒЁгҒҜзө№з№”зү©гҒ®дёҖзЁ®гғ»[[зөҒ|зөҒпјҲгҒӮгҒ—гҒҺгҒ¬пјү]]гӮ’жҢҮгҒҷиЁҖи‘үгҒ§гҖҒгҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁгҖҚгҒЁгҒҜгҒқгӮҢгӮ’з№”гӮӢжҠҖиЎ“иҖ…йӣҶеӣЈгӮ’иЎЁгҒҷгҖӮж–ҮзҢ®дёҠгҒ®й•·е№ЎйғЁж°ҸгҒ«гҒҜгҖҒзҡҮеҲҘж°Ҹж—ҸгҒЁжёЎжқҘзі»ж°Ҹж—ҸгҒҢиҰӢгӮүгӮҢгӮӢгҖӮгҖҺ[[ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ]]гҖҸйҖёж–ҮгҒ®[[йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|йҳҝжҷәзҺӢ]]жқЎгҒ§гҒҜгҖҒй•·е№ЎйғЁгҒ®зҘ–гҒҜеё°еҢ–гҒ—гҒҹгҖҢ[[дёғе§“жјўдәә]]гҖҚгҒ®гҒҶгҒЎзҡҖпјҲгҒ“гҒҶпјүе§“гҒ§гҖҒжң«иЈ”гҒ«дҪҗжіўеӨҡжқ‘дё»пјҲгҒ•гҒҜгҒҹгҒ®гҒҷгҒҗгӮҠпјүгҒҢгҒ„гӮӢгҒЁиЁҳгҒҷгҖӮгҒҫгҒҹгҖҺ[[еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ]]гҖҸ[[й–ӢеҢ–еӨ©зҡҮ]]ж®өгҒ«гӮҲгӮҢгҒ°гҖҒ[[еҪҰеқҗзҺӢ|ж—ҘеӯҗеқҗзҺӢ]]пјҲй–ӢеҢ–еӨ©зҡҮз¬¬3зҡҮеӯҗпјүгҒ®еӯҗгғ»[[зҘһеӨ§ж №зҺӢ]]пјҲгҒӢгӮҖгҒҠгҒҠгҒӯгҒ®гҒҚгҒҝпјүгҒҢй•·е№ЎйғЁгҒ®зҘ–гҒЁгҒ—гҖҒзҫҺжҝғгҒ®[[жң¬е·ЈеӣҪйҖ ]]гҒЁеҗҢж—ҸгҒ§гҒӮгӮӢгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҖӮ === еүөе»ә === еҪ“зӨҫгҒ®еүөе»әгҒ«гҒӨгҒ„гҒҰгҖҒгҖҺ[[еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ]]гҖҸ[[д№…ж…ҲйғЎ]]жқЎгҒ«гҒҜгҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁгҒ®зӨҫгҖҚгҒ«й–ўгҒҷгӮӢиЁҳдәӢгҒҢијүгӮӢгҖӮгҒ“гӮҢгҒ«гӮҲгӮӢгҒЁгҖҒзҸ еЈІзҫҺдёҮе‘ҪпјҲгҒҷгӮҒгҒҝгҒҫгҒ®гҒҝгҒ“гҒЁпјүзҡҮеӯ«пјҲгҒҷгӮҒгҒҝгҒҫпјүгҖҒгҒҷгҒӘгӮҸгҒЎ[[гғӢгғӢгӮ®|з“Ҡз“Ҡжқөе°Ҡ]]гӮ’жҢҮгҒҷгҖӮгҒҢеӨ©гҒӢгӮүйҷҚиҮЁгҒ—гҒҹйҡӣгҒ«з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘ҪгҒҢеҫ“гҒ„гҖҒ[[ж—Ҙеҗ‘еӣҪ|ж—Ҙеҗ‘]]гҒӢгӮү[[зҫҺжҝғеӣҪ|зҫҺжҝғ]]гҒ«иҮігҒЈгҒҹгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҖӮгҒқгҒ—гҒҰ[[еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ]]гҒ®еҫЎдё–гҒ«й•·е№ЎйғЁгҒ®йҒ зҘ–гғ»еӨҡеј–е‘ҪгҒҢзҫҺжҝғгҒӢгӮүд№…ж…ҲгҒ«йҒ·гӮҠгҖҒж©ҹж®ҝгӮ’е»әгҒҰгҒҰеҲқгӮҒгҒҰз№”гҒЈгҒҹгҒЁдјқгҒҲгӮӢгҖӮ еҪ“зӨҫгҒҜеҸӨеўізҫӨпјҲе№ЎеұұеҸӨеўізҫӨпјүгҒ®дёӯгҒ«дҪҚзҪ®гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒҢгҖҒгҒ“гҒ®еҸӨеўізҫӨгҒҜеҪ“зӨҫгҒ®зҘӯзҘҖж°Ҹж—ҸгҒ®гӮӮгҒ®гҒЁиҰӢгӮүгӮҢгҖҒгҒқгҒ®еҮәеңҹе“ҒгҒӢгӮүеҪ“зӨҫгҒӘгӮүгҒігҒ«зҘӯзҘҖж°Ҹж—ҸгҒ®жҖ§ж јгҒҢжҢҮж‘ҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖӮ === жҰӮеҸІ === гҖҺеёёйҷёиӘҢж–ҷйғЎйғ·иҖғгҖҸгҒ«гӮҲгӮҢгҒ°гҖҒ[[зҘһйҡҺ]]гҒҜ[[д»ҒеҜҝ]]е…ғе№ҙпјҲ[[851е№ҙ]]пјүгҒ«жӯЈе…ӯдҪҚдёҠгҖҺж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІгҖҸд»ҒеҜҝе…ғе№ҙпјҲ851е№ҙпјүжӯЈжңҲ27ж—ҘжқЎгҒ«гҒҜгҖҒеӨ©дёӢгҒ®и«ёзҘһгҒ«еҜҫгҒ—гҒҰжңүдҪҚз„ЎдҪҚгӮ’е•ҸгӮҸгҒҡжӯЈе…ӯдҪҚдёҠгҒ«еҸҷгҒҷгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶиЁҳдәӢгӮ’ијүгҒӣгӮӢгҖӮгҖҒгҒ®гҒЎ10еәҰгҒ®иҙҲдҪҚгҒ«гӮҲгӮҠ[[жҳҺеҝң]]10е№ҙпјҲ[[1501е№ҙ]]пјүгҒ«жӯЈдёүдҪҚгҒ«йҒ”гҒ—гҒҹгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҖҺиҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®ең°еҗҚгҖҸй•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫй …гҖӮгҖӮ[[е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј]]дёӯжңҹгҒ®гҖҺ[[е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі]]гҖҸгҒ«гҒҜгҖҢ[[еёёйҷёеӣҪ]]д№…ж…ҲйғЎ й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁиЁҳијүгҒ•гӮҢгҖҒ[[ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ]]гҒ«еҲ—гҒ—гҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҒӘгҒҠгҖҒгҖҺе»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёігҖҸгҒ§гҒҜ[[жӯҰи”өеӣҪ]][[иіҖзҫҺйғЎ]]гҒ«гӮӮеҗҢеҗҚгҒ®[[й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ]]гӮ’ијүгҒӣгӮӢгҖӮгҖӮеёёйҷёеӣҪгҒЁй•·е№ЎйғЁгҒ®й–ўдҝӮгҒ«й–ўгҒ—гҒҰгҒҜгҖҒгҖҺ[[е»¶е–ңејҸ]]гҖҸдё»иЁҲејҸгҒ«гҒҜеёёйҷёеӣҪгҒ®иӘҝгҒЁгҒ—гҒҰгҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁ7з–ӢгҖҚгҒ®иЁҳијүгҒҢгҒӮгӮӢгҒ»гҒӢгҖҒгҖҺ[[йЎһиҒҡеӣҪеҸІ]]гҖҸе·»54гҒ§[[ејҳд»Ғ]]8е№ҙпјҲ[[817е№ҙ]]пјүгҒ«гҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁзҰҸиүҜеҘігҖҚгҒ®еҗҚгҒҢиҰӢгҒҲгӮӢгҖҺж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ…гҖҸй•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫй …гҖӮгҖӮ [[еә·е№і]]е№ҙй–“пјҲ[[1058е№ҙ]]-[[1065е№ҙ]]пјүгҒ«[[жәҗй јзҫ©]]гҒҢеҘҘзҫҪеҮәе…өгҒ®йҡӣгҒ«жҲҺж——1ж——гӮ’еҘүзҙҚгҒ—гҒҰжҲҰеӢқзҘҲйЎҳгӮ’иЎҢгҒ„гҖҒеҮұж—ӢжҷӮгҒ«й№ҝеі¶гғ»дёүеі¶гғ»зҘһжҳҺгғ»иӢҘе®®гҒ®гҖҢеӣӣжүҖжҳҺзҘһгҖҚгӮ’еӢ§и«ӢгҒ—гҒҹгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҖӮгҒ“гҒ®еӣӣжүҖжҳҺзҘһгҒҢзӣӣеӨ§гҒ«гҒӘгҒЈгҒҰгҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒ®зӨҫеҸ·гӮ’еӨұгҒ„гҖҒгҒ•гӮүгҒ«гҒ®гҒЎгҒ«гҒҜгҖҢй№ҝеі¶жҳҺзҘһгҖҚгҒЁз§°гҒҷгӮӢгҒ®гҒҝгҒ«гҒӘгҒЈгҒҹгҒЁдјқгҒҲгӮӢгҖӮ [[дёӯдё–]]д»ҘйҷҚгҒҜгҖҢе°Ҹе№Ўи¶іжҳҺзҘһгҖҚгҒ®гҒЎгҖҢй§’еҪўзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒЁгҒ„гҒЈгҒҹгҖӮ[[е»¶дә«]]е№ҙй–“пјҲ[[1744е№ҙ]]-[[1748е№ҙ]]пјүгҒ«иҮігӮҠгҖҒеҸӨиҖҒгҒ®еҸЈзў‘гҒ«гӮҲгӮҠж—§еҸ·гҒ®гҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫгҖҚгҒ«еҫ©гҒ—гҒҹгҖӮеҪ“зӨҫгҒҜ[[ж°ҙжҲёи—©]]гҒӢгӮүгҒ®еҙҮж•¬гӮӮзҜӨгҒҸгҖҒйҷӨең°4зҹігҒҢиЁұгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гҒҹгҖӮ [[жҳҺжІ»]]6е№ҙпјҲ[[1873е№ҙ]]пјүгҖҒ[[иҝ‘д»ЈзӨҫж јеҲ¶еәҰ]]гҒ«гҒҠгҒ„гҒҰ[[йғ·зӨҫ]]гҒ«еҲ—гҒ—гҒҹгҖӮ == й–ўдҝӮең° == * ж—§е®®и·Ў *: еҪ“зӨҫгҒ®ж—§зӨҫең°гҒ§гҖҒеҢ—иҘҝзҙ„700гғЎгғјгғҲгғ«гҒ«дҪҚзҪ®гҒҷгӮӢгҖӮзҘһијҝгҒҢеҮәгӮӢйҡӣгҒҜеҝ…гҒҡгҒ“гҒ“гҒ«е®үзҪ®гҒҷгӮӢгҒЁгҒ„гҒҶгҖӮ * е№ЎеұұеҸӨеўізҫӨ *: еҪ“зӨҫгҒ®йҺ®еә§гҒҷгӮӢеҸ°ең°дёҠгҒ«гҒӮгӮӢеҸӨеўізҫӨгҒ§гҖҒ10ж•°еҹәгҒ®еҸӨеўігҒӢгӮүгҒӘгӮӢгҖӮеӨҡгҒҸгҒ®еүҜи‘¬е“ҒгҒҢеҮәеңҹгҒ—гҒҰгҒҠгӮҠгҖҒеёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®еҸІи·ЎгҒ«жҢҮе®ҡгҒ•гӮҢгҒҰгҒ„гӮӢгҖӮеҪ“зӨҫгӮ’еҘүж–ҺгҒ—гҒҹж°Ҹж—ҸгҒЁгҒ®й–ўдҝӮгҒҢжҢҮж‘ҳгҒ•гӮҢгӮӢгҖҺиҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®ең°еҗҚгҖҸе№ЎеұұеҸӨеўізҫӨй …гҖӮгҖӮ == зҘӯдәӢ == * е…ғж—ҰзҘӯ пјҲ1жңҲ1ж—ҘпјүзҘӯдәӢгҒҜзҘһзӨҫз”ұз·’жӣёгҒ«гӮҲгӮӢгҖӮ * зҘҲе№ҙзҘӯ пјҲ2жңҲ20ж—Ҙпјү * жҳҘдҫӢзҘӯ пјҲ4жңҲ9ж—Ҙпјү * з§ӢеӯЈзҘӯ пјҲж—§жҡҰ9жңҲ29ж—Ҙпјү * ж–°еҳ—зҘӯ пјҲ11жңҲ30ж—Ҙпјү == зҸҫең°жғ…е ұ == '''жүҖеңЁең°''' * [[иҢЁеҹҺзңҢ]][[еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ]]е№Ўз”ә539 '''е‘Ёиҫә''' * е№ЎеұұеҸӨеўізҫӨ == и„ҡжіЁ == {{и„ҡжіЁгғҳгғ«гғ—}} '''жіЁйҮҲ''' {{reflist|group="жіЁ"}} '''еҮәе…ё''' еҢ—з• е…ӢзҫҺгҖҢй•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫгҖҚпјҲ[[и°·е·қеҒҘдёҖ]] з·ЁгҖҺж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°- 11 й–ўжқұгҖҸпјҲ[[зҷҪж°ҙзӨҫ]]гҖҒ1984е№ҙпјүпјү * гҖҺж—Ҙжң¬еҸӨд»Јж°Ҹж—ҸдәәеҗҚиҫһе…ё жҷ®еҸҠзүҲгҖҸпјҲ[[еҗүе·қејҳж–ҮйӨЁ]]гҖҒ2010е№ҙпјүй•·е№ЎйғЁж°Ҹй … == й–ўйҖЈй …зӣ® == {{Commonscat|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}} * [[й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ]] пјҲ[[еҹјзҺүзңҢ]][[е…җзҺүйғЎ]][[дёҠйҮҢз”ә]]пјү - [[жӯҰи”өеӣҪ]][[иіҖзҫҺйғЎ]]гҒ®ејҸеҶ…зӨҫгҖӮ {{зҘһйҒ“ жЁӘ}}{{иҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®зҘһзӨҫдёҖиҰ§зӣ®ж¬Ў}} {{DEFAULTSORT:гҒӘгҒӢгҒҜгҒҹгҒёгҒ—гӮ“гҒ—гӮ„}} {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}}

In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]]

{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinai-sha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{DEFAULTSORT:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Former Village Shrines]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]]

Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»жҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]]

{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]]

{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]]

It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:иҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®ж—§йғ·зӨҫ]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]]

{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:иҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®ж—§йғ·зӨҫ]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}}

It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:иҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®ж—§йғ·зӨҫ]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}}
