In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:иҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®ж—§йғ·зӨҫ]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}}

{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:иҢЁеҹҺзңҢгҒ®ж—§йғ·зӨҫ]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}}

It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®е»әзҜүзү©]] [[Category:еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮгҒ®жӯҙеҸІ]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}}

In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Architecture of HitachiЕҚta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}}

revision.text:
{{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[:en:Hitachiota|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiota|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Architecture of HitachiЕҚta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}}

Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left | prefecture = {{ill|Hitachiota|qid=Q838663}} }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiota|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Architecture of HitachiЕҚta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}}

Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left | location = {{ill|Hitachiota|qid=Q838663}} }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiota|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=|1=Ashiginu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257295}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Architecture of HitachiЕҚta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}} [[Category:qq]]

Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left | location = {{ill|Hitachiota|qid=Q838663}} }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiota|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=|1=Ashiginu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257295}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Architecture of HitachiЕҚta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left | location = {{ill|Hitachiota|qid=Q838663}} }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiota|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=|1=Ashiginu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257295}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Architecture of HitachiЕҚta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left | location = {{ill|Hitachiota|qid=Q838663}} }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiota|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=|1=Ashiginu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257295}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Architecture of HitachiЕҚta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left | location = {{ill|Hitachiota|qid=Q838663}} }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiota|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=|1=Ashiginu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257295}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Architecture of HitachiЕҚta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}} {{wikidata link|Q17227113}}

Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left | location = {{ill|Hitachiota|WD=Q838663}} }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiota|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=|1=Ashiginu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257295}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Architecture of HitachiЕҚta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}} {{wikidata link|Q17227113}}

Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left | location = {{ill|Hitachiota|WD=Q838663}} }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Hitachiota|Hitachiota]], [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ|2=ja|3=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|4=zh|5=е»¶е–ңејҸзҘһеҗҚеёі|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|1=Ashiginu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11257295}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the [[:en:Nihon Shoki|Nihon Shoki]] and [[:en:Kojiki|Kojiki]] indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the [[:en:Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku]], one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by [[:en:Achi no omi|Achi no Omi]]. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|1=Hitachi no Kuni Fudoki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11481687}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied [[:en:Ninigi-no-Mikoto|Ninigi]] on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the [[:en:Nihon Montoku TennЕҚ Jitsuroku|Jinshin Jitsuroku]], the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in [[:en:Ninju|Ninju]] 1 (851). The [[:en:Engishiki|Engishiki]] lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under [[:en:Kuji District, Ibaraki|Kujigun]] in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the [[:en:Heian period|Heian period]], [[:en:Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi]] prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the [[:en:Meiji era|Meiji period]], the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|1=Nihon rekishi chimei taikei|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q17213509}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Architecture of HitachiЕҚta City]] [[Category:Municipal History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Former GЕҚ-sha in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] {{Commons category|Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)}} {{wikidata link|Q17227113}}

It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]]

Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]
