Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]

Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]

Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == [[Category:Pages with merged translations]][[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]

It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]==Notes==

Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] ==Notes== [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]]

It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]

Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]

It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]

It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)

It is located in [[Hitachi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]

Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[sn:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] ==Notes== [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]]

revision.text:
'''Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki JinmyЕҚchЕҚ]]. It is located in [[Hitachi Province]]. == Shikinaisha (ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Shrine DB |- | {{ill|Kuji District, Ibaraki|en|Kuji District, Ibaraki|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|ceb|Kuji-gun|es|Distrito de Kuji (Ibaraki)|fr|District de Kuji|id|Distrik Kuji, Ibaraki|it|Distretto di Kuji|ko|кө¬м§Җкө°|pl|Powiat Kuji|th|аёӯаёіа№Җаё аёӯаё„аё№аёҲаёҙ|uk|РҹРҫРІС–СӮ РҡСғРҙР·С–|vi|Kuji, Ibaraki|zh|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|lt=Kuji district|WD=Q1075212}} || {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)|lt=Nagahatabe Shrine|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|commons|Category:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)|WD=Q17227113}} || {{ill|Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)|WD=Q135160342|lt=Kokuhei|comment=categorized}} || {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|WD=Q134917287}} || вҖ” || вҖ” || {{coord|36.536129|140.545339|display=inline}} || [https://jmapps.ne.jp/kokugakuin/det.html?data_id=181759 DB] |} {{wikidata link|Q17227113}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]
Haiden | location = 539 Hata-cho, [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]] | coordinates = {{Coord|36|32|9|N|140|32|43|E|landmark_region:JP-08|display=inline,title}} | deity = {{Ublist|Kihime-no-Mikoto|Tate-no-Mikoto}} | Sacred Object = | Shrine Rank = [[Shikinaisha|Shikinaisha]] (minor)
Former [[GЕҚsha|village shrine]] | Foundation = (Tradition) During the reign of the 10th emperor, [[Emperor Sujin]] | Main Hall = [[Nagare-zukuri]] | alternate_name = | Annual festival = April 9 | Rituals = | Map = Japan Ibaraki | Map label position = left }} [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) torii.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Torii]] [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) honden.JPG|thumb|200px|right|Honden]] '''Nagahatabe Shrine''' (й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ, Nagahatabe Jinja) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|зҘһзӨҫ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in [[Hitachiota]], [[Ibaraki Prefecture]]. It is listed as a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=ејҸеҶ…зӨҫ|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=}} and was Ranked as a village shrine under the [[modern shrine Ranking system]]. == Deities == The shrine enshrines the following two deities, considered ancestral gods of the Nagahatabe clan according to the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}Shrine's historical documents.: * '''Kihime-no-Mikoto''' (з¶әж—ҘеҘіе‘Ҫ) * '''Tate-no-Mikoto''' (еӨҡеј–е‘Ҫ) == History == === About Nagahatabe === The term "Nagahata" refers to a type of silk fabric called {{ill|Ashiginu|ja|зөҒ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ashiginu|qq=}}, and the "Nagahatabe" represents a group of artisans skilled in weaving this fabric. Historical records such as the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӣёзҙҖ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nihon Shoki|qq=}} and {{ill|Kojiki|ja|еҸӨдәӢиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kojiki|qq=}} indicate that the Nagahatabe clan may have roots in both imperial and immigrant lineages. According to the {{ill|Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|ja|ж–°ж’°е§“ж°ҸйҢІ|1=Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinsen ShЕҚjiroku|qq=}}, one lineage traces its origin to the immigrant "Seven Surnamed Han People" led by {{ill|Achi no Omi|ja|йҳҝзҹҘдҪҝдё»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Achi no Omi|qq=}}. === Foundation === The shrine's foundation is described in the {{ill|Hitachi Fudoki|ja|еёёйҷёеӣҪйўЁеңҹиЁҳ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hitachi Fudoki|qq=}}, which states that Kihime-no-Mikoto accompanied {{ill|Ninigi|ja|гғӢгғӢгӮ®|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} on his descent from the heavens and later moved to Hitachi under the guidance of Tate-no-Mikoto during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|ja|еҙҮзҘһеӨ©зҡҮ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Sujin|qq=}}. It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Shrine | name = Nagahatabe Shrine | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = [[File:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota) haiden.JPG|280px]]

It is believed that Tate-no-Mikoto established a weaving facility and began the practice of weaving at this location. === Later History === According to the {{ill|Jinshin Jitsuroku|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬ж–ҮеҫіеӨ©зҡҮе®ҹйҢІ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jinshin Jitsuroku|qq=}}, the shrine received a Rank of Senior Sixth Rank Upper in {{ill|Ninju|ja|д»ҒеҜҝ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninju|qq=}} 1 (851). The {{ill|Engishiki|ja|е»¶е–ңејҸ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki|qq=}} lists it as "Nagahatabe Shrine" under {{ill|Kujigun|ja|д№…ж…ҲйғЎ|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kujigun|qq=}} in Hitachi ProvinceAnother shrine with the same name, Nagahatabe Shrine, is listed in Musashi Province.. During the {{ill|Heian period|ja|е№іе®үжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heian period|qq=}}, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|ja|жәҗй јзҫ©|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minamoto no Yoriyoshi|qq=}} prayed for victory at this shrine during his campaign and dedicated a flag. Later, the shrine's prominence declined, and it was renamed several times, including "Komaata Shrine." In the {{ill|Edo period|ja|жұҹжҲёжҷӮд»Ј|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Edo period|qq=}}, the shrine was restored to its original name "Nagahatabe Shrine" based on oral traditions. In the {{ill|Meiji period|ja|жҳҺжІ»|lt=Meiji period|1=Meiji era|2=ja|3=жҳҺжІ»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji period|qq=}}, the shrine was Ranked as a village shrine in the modern shrine Ranking system. == Related Sites == * '''Former Shrine Site:''' Located about 700 meters northwest of the current location. * '''Hata Yama Kofun Cluster:''' A group of Kofuns on the shrine's grounds, designated as a historic site by Hitachiota City. == Festivals == The shrine holds several annual festivals, including: * '''New Year Festival''' (January 1)Shrine's historical documents. * '''Prayer Festival''' (February 20) * '''Spring Festival''' (April 9) * '''Autumn Festival''' (Lunisolar September 29) * '''Harvest Festival''' (November 30) == Current Information == '''Address:''' 539 Hata-cho, Hitachiota, Ibaraki Prefecture '''Nearby:''' * Hata Yama Kofun Cluster == Notes == {{notelist}} == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == * Shrine historical documents and site plaques * "Nagahatabe Shrine" in {{ill|Japanese Place name Dictionary|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬жӯҙеҸІең°еҗҚеӨ§зі»|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Place name Dictionary|qq=}} (Heibonsha) * Katsumi Kitabatake, "Nagahatabe Shrine," in {{ill|The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|ja|ж—Ҙжң¬гҒ®зҘһгҖ… -зҘһзӨҫгҒЁиҒ–ең°-|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/The Gods of Japan - Shrines and Sacred Sites|qq=}} Vol. 11, Kanto (Hakusuisha, 1984) == See Also == * {{ill|Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|en|Nagahatabe Shrine (дёҠйҮҢз”ә)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nagahatabe Shrine (Kamisato)|qq=}} {{Shinto Horizontal}}{{Ibaraki Shrine Index}} {{translated page|ja|й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)|version=86946502|comment=Imported full ja history}} ==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Nagahatabe-jinja (Hitachiota)]] [[en:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[ja:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Accidentally overwritten pages]] [[Category:Buildings in Hitachiota]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:History of Hitachiota]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha (Engishiki Jinmyocho)]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Nagahatabe Shrine (Hitachiota)]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:pages that were shrank]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Hitachi Province]] [[Category:Shrines in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Son-sha]] [[Category:й•·е№ЎйғЁзҘһзӨҫ (еёёйҷёеӨӘз”°еёӮ)]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]]
